response: {"calibration_score":0.52,"overconfident_count":3,"underconfident_count":0,"well_calibrated_count":16,"biases_detected":["path_existence_anchoring: units 6ee158bd-3 and 6345a8d3-12 assumed file paths existed before verifying, causing initial tool failures before recovery","tool_language_anchoring: unit 6ee158bd-4 attempted python3 invocation in a Node.js-primary project context before switching to node, suggesting anchoring to general scripting defaults over project-local conventions","redundant_read_pattern: unit 6345a8d3-21 reads the same PLAN.md file twice consecutively with no intervening writes, indicating possible context retrieval uncertainty rather than deliberate re-check","scope_expansion_bias: unit 0467461b-7 created 10 new files and deleted existing ones in a single unit without an explicit planning step (no EnterPlanMode), contrasted with unit 6ee158bd-8 which correctly entered plan mode for a comparably scoped change"],"recommendations":["Add existence pre-checks (Glob or Bash ls) before Read or multi-path Bash commands when paths are derived from memory or prior session context rather than observed tool results","Prefer project-local runtime (node, npx) over generic interpreters (python3) when the working directory contains a package.json — detect this from session context before issuing shell commands","When a single execution unit will create or delete more than 3 files, require an explicit plan step (EnterPlanMode or TodoWrite) to reduce scope-expansion drift","Treat consecutive reads of the same file within one unit as a signal of context loss — consider writing a scratchpad note instead of re-reading to preserve token efficiency and reduce redundancy"]}
